Identity.History: A strategos was a military general in ancient Greece, especially important in Athens. Elected by citizens, strategoi commanded armies and fleets, planned campaigns, managed logistics, and often influenced foreign policy. Unlike many officials chosen by lot, they were selected for skill and experience and could be re-elected. Famous strategoi included Pericles, Themistocles, and Alcibiades. The office combined military command with political authority, making it one of the most powerful roles in a Greek city-state.
